mistake: .gitignore exposed internal project structure in public GitHub repo - revealed other platforms (twitter, discord, HN), planning doc patterns (*PLAN*.md, REDDIT_APPLICATION*.md), and internal framework details (.empirica/)
why_wrong: Misunderstood .gitignore purpose - thought it was for privacy control, but it's a PUBLIC tracked file visible to everyone on GitHub. .gitignore prevents accidental commits, NOT information disclosure
cost_estimate: 2 hours to detect + fix (nuclear repo rebuild). Could have leaked project scope to Reddit API reviewers
prevention: ALWAYS use generic .gitignore patterns. Never list specific internal folders/files that reveal project scope. Remember: Everything in git is public (files, history, messages, .gitignore). For true privacy, don't commit at all

mistake: Statusline showed data from wrong tmux pane due to stale test sessions with instance_id=NULL being matched by prefix fallback
why_wrong: Priority 3 prefix match (ai_id LIKE 'claude-code%') combined with instance_id IS NULL fallback allowed test sessions like claude-code-test to match when no exact session found
prevention: 1) Always close test sessions after testing, 2) Consider tightening instance_id IS NULL fallback to only match when explicitly requested, 3) Add cleanup script for orphaned test sessions

mistake: Spent hours debugging Tailscale SSH ACL rules when the actual blocker was the ts-input iptables chain DROP rule. We assumed ACL changes would propagate to the local firewall for shared nodes.
why_wrong: Tailscale SSH ACL is a separate system from regular SSH over Tailscale. The ts-input iptables chain is the LOCAL enforcement layer and doesn't auto-populate per-peer ACCEPT rules for cross-tailnet shared nodes. We should have checked iptables first when tcpdump showed zero inbound packets.
prevention: When Tailscale connectivity fails: (1) FIRST check `sudo iptables -L ts-input -n` for DROP rules, (2) tcpdump showing zero packets = local filter problem not ACL problem, (3) Don't conflate Tailscale SSH (managed SSH) with regular SSH over Tailscale tunnel
mistake_data: {"mistake": "Spent hours debugging Tailscale SSH ACL rules when the actual blocker was the ts-input iptables chain DROP rule. We assumed ACL changes would propagate to the local firewall for shared nodes.", "why_wrong": "Tailscale SSH ACL is a separate system from regular SSH over Tailscale. The ts-input iptables chain is the LOCAL enforcement layer and doesn't auto-populate per-peer ACCEPT rules for cross-tailnet shared nodes. We should have checked iptables first when tcpdump showed zero inbound packets.", "cost_estimate": "3 hours", "root_cause_vector": "CONTEXT", "prevention": "When Tailscale connectivity fails: (1) FIRST check `sudo iptables -L ts-input -n` for DROP rules, (2) tcpdump showing zero packets = local filter problem not ACL problem, (3) Don't conflate Tailscale SSH (managed SSH) with regular SSH over Tailscale tunnel"}

mistake: Used Claude Code built-in Task agents for parallel investigation instead of Empirica's own epistemic agent system (agent-spawn/agent-parallel). The built-in agents don't track epistemic state, don't link to sessions, and don't produce structured noetic artifacts.
why_wrong: Empirica's agent system exists precisely for this use case — parallel investigation with epistemic tracking. Using generic agents bypasses the framework we're building and loses the meta-learning data. The results were useful but unstructured and not linked to the session's epistemic graph.
prevention: When spawning investigation agents, always use Empirica's agent-spawn or agent-parallel commands. Reserve Claude Code's Task tool for non-epistemic work (file operations, simple searches).

mistake: Conflated MANAGE pages with OBSERVE pages — applied per-project db_path scoping to System, Sentinel, Memory, Injection pages when they should be OS-level configuration management pages that read from config files, env vars, and shared services (Qdrant), not per-project SQLite databases.
why_wrong: Assumed all dashboard pages follow the same data model. Didn't investigate what system_dashboard.py actually exposes. Extrapolated the per-project pattern to pages that have a fundamentally different purpose: configuration management vs data visualization.
prevention: Always investigate the actual source/architecture before applying patterns. MANAGE pages = config files + env vars + shared services. OBSERVE pages = per-project SQLite. These are orthogonal data sources.

mistake: Conflated learning trajectory with capability belief - fed learning deltas to Sentinel as bias corrections
why_wrong: Learning grows naturally over time; capability belief should be stable. Using learning as calibration = more learning means more correction - backwards.
prevention: Ask: is this LEARNING or CALIBRATION? Learning=track only. Calibration=grounded in evidence.
mistake_data: {"mistake": "Conflated learning trajectory with capability belief - fed learning deltas to Sentinel as bias corrections", "why_wrong": "Learning grows naturally over time; capability belief should be stable. mistake: Used pwd instead of empirica project-switch when user explicitly asked to switch projects
why_wrong: Ignored CLAUDE.md workflow instructions. pwd shows current directory but doesn't activate project context or database. project-switch loads project context, attaches session, and sets database scope.
prevention: When user says 'project switch to X' or 'switch to X project', always use 'empirica project-switch <name>' command, not pwd or cd

mistake: Used intuitive trigger names (new/fresh) instead of verifying Claude Code's actual SessionStart trigger values
why_wrong: Claude Code documentation specifies exact trigger strings: startup, resume, clear, compact. Using guessed names meant session-init NEVER fired from hooks - only from manual invocation.
prevention: Always verify hook trigger values against platform documentation. Add a test that fires each hook matcher against known triggers to confirm at least one match.

mistake: Claimed source ~/.bashrc would fix EMPIRICA_SENTINEL_LOOPING in current session. Each Bash tool call is a subprocess that inherits from the Claude Code parent process. source/export in a subprocess cannot modify the parent's environment. Session restart required.
why_wrong: Didn't account for the subprocess model of the Bash tool - env changes in child processes don't propagate to the parent Claude Code process
prevention: Remember: Bash tool calls are subprocesses. Environment variable changes require session restart to take effect in Claude Code's parent process and its hook subprocesses.

Session restart required.", "why_wrong": "Didn't account for the subprocess model of the Bash tool - env changes in child processes don't propagate to the parent Claude Code process", "cost_estimate": null, "root_cause_vector": null, "prevention": "Remember: Bash tool calls are subprocesses. Environment variable changes require session restart to take effect in Claude Code's parent process and its hook subprocesses.", "transaction_id": "b2769189-e148-4418-8967-72f585370fbd"}

mistake: Removed typing.Optional import from transaction-enforcer.py but _find_transaction_file() still used Optional[Path] in its return type annotation
why_wrong: py_compile doesn't evaluate type annotations at module level in Python 3.10+ — the NameError only fires at function call time. Smoke test only tested project_resolver imports, not each hook end-to-end.
prevention: After removing imports, grep for all uses of the removed symbol in the file, not just the function being deleted
